suspects.name: Mrs. Eliza Jones
suspects.deception_policy: Mrs. Jones will lie about her whereabouts to protect herself and avoid suspicion.

suspects.name: Mr. Thomas Brown
suspects.deception_policy: Mr. Brown will be truthful to avoid implicating himself unnecessarily.

suspects.name: Dr. William Smith
suspects.deception_policy: Dr. Smith will remain neutral and not incriminate himself if there is any doubt.

suspects.name: Miss Emily Smythe
suspects.deception_policy: Miss Smythe will be honest as she has nothing to hide.

suspects.name: Mr. Henry Green
suspects.deception_policy: Mr. Green will be truthful but keep to himself.

locations.name: Study
locations.description: A dark, cluttered room with a large oak desk and shelves filled with books.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: An expansive room lined with floor-to-ceiling bookshelves and comfortable armchairs.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.description: A spacious, well-equipped kitchen with a large central island and antique appliances.

locations.name: Bedroom
locations.description: Lady Margaret's private room, decorated in rich fabrics and elegant furnishings.

locations.name: Garden
locations.description: A beautifully manicured garden with a winding path and a small fountain.

locations.name: Conservatory
locations.description: A glass-enclosed room filled with exotic plants and comfortable seating.

clues.description: A threatening letter addressed to Lady Margaret, dated the day of her death.
An open book on the desk with a bookmark pointing to a page describing poison.
A blood-stained knife hidden under the sink.
An unlabeled vial of clear liquid on Lady Margaret's dresser.
A garden spade with mud caked around the handle.

canonical_timeline.description: Miss Emily Smythe arrives at the library to retrieve a book.
Mrs. Eliza Jones is last seen entering the kitchen with a suspicious look on her face.
Lady Margaret Hargrove is found dead in the study, poisoned by an unknown substance.
Dr. William Smith enters Lady Margaret's bedroom to perform a routine checkup.
